intro: Canaanite in the Gospels designates Simon, one of the twelve apostles, called Simon the Canaanite (Matthew 10:4; Mark 3:18) to distinguish him from Simon Peter. The term does not indicate Canaanite ethnic origin but is a transliteration of the Aramaic qan'ana, meaning zealot — the same epithet rendered as "the Zealot" in Luke 6:15 and Acts 1:13. Simon may have been a member of the Zealot movement, a Jewish faction committed to armed resistance against Roman occupation, or the title may simply reflect personal zeal. Beyond his listing in the apostolic roll calls, no further narrative about him survives in the New Testament.
